title: Add minimize/maximize declarations to grammar and compiler
analysis: The grammar currently has 4 member declaration types in _member (param, let, constraint, sub) at grammar.js:35-40. The keywords 'minimize' and 'maximize' are not recognized as declaration-level constructs, so they silently misparse as identifier expressions. The constraint solver already supports OptimizationObjective::Minimize/Maximize (reify-types/src/constraint.rs:46-53) via ResolutionProblem.objective, but there's no path from source code to that type. The fix threads minimize/maximize through three layers: (1) Grammar: add minimize_declaration and maximize_declaration rules following the constraint_declaration pattern (keyword + expression). (2) Syntax: add MinimizeDecl/MaximizeDecl structs and MemberDecl variants, plus CST→AST lowering methods mirroring lower_constraint(). (3) Compiler: add 'objective: Option<OptimizationObjective>' field to TopologyTemplate, handle new MemberDecl variants in compile_structure() by compiling the expression and wrapping in the appropriate OptimizationObjective variant. TopologyTemplate is only directly constructed in compile_structure() (lib.rs:805) and TopologyTemplateBuilder::build() (builders.rs:260), keeping the structural change contained. The eval layer currently hardcodes 'objective: None' when building ResolutionProblem — wiring objectives through is out of scope for this task.

prerequisites.description: Add minimize_declaration and maximize_declaration rules to grammar.js. Add $.minimize_declaration and $.maximize_declaration to the _member choice list (lines 35-40). Define each as seq(keyword, field('expr', $._expression)) following the constraint_declaration pattern (lines 65-68). minimize_declaration uses 'minimize', maximize_declaration uses 'maximize'.

Regenerate tree-sitter parser by running 'npx --yes tree-sitter-cli generate' in the tree-sitter-reify directory. This updates src/parser.c, src/grammar.json, and src/node-types.json to include the new minimize_declaration and maximize_declaration node types. Verify the generated node-types.json contains both new node types.

steps.description: Write failing parse tests in crates/reify-syntax/src/ts_parser.rs #[cfg(test)] module: (a) parse_minimize_declaration: parse a structure containing 'minimize volume' and assert the resulting members include a MemberDecl::Minimize variant with expr ExprKind::Ident("volume"). (b) parse_maximize_declaration: parse a structure containing 'maximize thickness' and assert MemberDecl::Maximize with expr ExprKind::Ident("thickness"). Both tests fail to compile because MinimizeDecl/MaximizeDecl types and MemberDecl::Minimize/Maximize variants do not exist yet.

Implement AST types and CST→AST lowering for minimize/maximize: (1) In crates/reify-syntax/src/lib.rs, add MinimizeDecl struct with fields {expr: Expr, span: SourceSpan, content_hash: ContentHash} and identical MaximizeDecl struct. Add MemberDecl::Minimize(MinimizeDecl) and MemberDecl::Maximize(MaximizeDecl) variants. (2) In ts_parser.rs lower_structure() (lines 113-162), add match arms for 'minimize_declaration' and 'maximize_declaration' following the constraint_declaration pattern: error check → lower → push to members. (3) Add lower_minimize() and lower_maximize() methods following lower_constraint() pattern (lines 240-250): extract 'expr' field via child_by_field_name, call lower_expr(), construct MinimizeDecl/MaximizeDecl with span and content_hash. (4) Update any exhaustive match expressions on MemberDecl in existing tests (spans_are_valid, content_hashes) to handle the new variants. Step-1 tests should now pass.

Write additional parse validation tests: (a) parse_minimize_complex_expression: parse 'minimize width * height' → assert expr is ExprKind::BinOp with op '*'. (b) parse_minimize_with_other_members: parse a structure with params, lets, constraints, and 'minimize thickness' → assert correct member count and that minimize is present alongside other members. (c) minimize_span_and_hash: verify span covers full 'minimize <expr>' text and content_hash matches source text. These should all pass after step-2.

Write failing compile test in crates/reify-compiler/tests/boundary2_producer.rs: compile_minimize_objective: parse source 'structure S { param x: Scalar = auto\n constraint x > 2mm\n minimize x }' → compile → assert template.objective is Some(OptimizationObjective::Minimize(expr)) where the expr references ValueCellId("S", "x"). This fails to compile because TopologyTemplate has no 'objective' field.

Add objective support to compiler and builder: (1) In crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs, add 'pub objective: Option<reify_types::OptimizationObjective>' field to TopologyTemplate struct after constraints (line 32). Import OptimizationObjective from reify_types. (2) In compile_structure(), initialize 'let mut objective: Option<OptimizationObjective> = None'. Add match arms for MemberDecl::Minimize/Maximize: compile expr via compile_expr(), wrap in OptimizationObjective::Minimize/Maximize, assign to objective. Include 'objective' in the TopologyTemplate construction (line 805). (3) In crates/reify-test-support/src/builders.rs, add 'objective: Option<reify_types::OptimizationObjective>' to TopologyTemplateBuilder (default None). Add '.objective(obj: reify_types::OptimizationObjective) -> Self' method. Include field in build(). Step-4 test should now pass.

Write compile test for maximize: compile_maximize_objective: parse source with 'maximize volume' where volume is a let binding → compile → assert template.objective is Some(OptimizationObjective::Maximize(expr)). Also write backward compatibility test: compile bracket_source() (no minimize/maximize) → assert template.objective is None, and all existing constraints/value_cells are still correct. Both should pass after step-5.

Write end-to-end round-trip integration test in boundary2_producer.rs: define source string with structure containing auto params, constraints, let bindings, and 'minimize thickness' → parse via reify_syntax::parse() → compile via reify_compiler::compile() → verify: (a) no error diagnostics, (b) correct value_cells count, (c) correct constraints count, (d) template.objective is Some(Minimize(...)), (e) the compiled expression inside the objective references the correct ValueCellId for thickness, (f) existing constraints and value cells are unaffected. This validates the full parse→compile pipeline.

Run full 'cargo test' across the workspace to verify no regressions. Fix any compilation or test failures introduced by the new MemberDecl variants (exhaustive match patterns in other crates), the new TopologyTemplate field, or grammar changes. Ensure all 365+ existing tests continue to pass alongside the new tests.

design_decisions.decision: Use two separate AST types (MinimizeDecl, MaximizeDecl) rather than a single ObjectiveDecl with direction enum
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the task description exactly ('add MinimizeDecl/MaximizeDecl to MemberDecl enum') and mirrors the grammar structure where minimize_declaration and maximize_declaration are separate rules. Consistent with how constraint_declaration is its own distinct type. Keeps the AST closely aligned with the CST for clarity.

design_decisions.decision: MinimizeDecl and MaximizeDecl have fields {expr, span, content_hash} — same as ConstraintDecl minus the label
design_decisions.rationale: The grammar syntax is identical to constraint (keyword + expression). Labels on objectives don't make semantic sense. Fields match the existing pattern for all member declarations (every member decl has span and content_hash).

design_decisions.decision: TopologyTemplate.objective is Option<OptimizationObjective> (singular optional, not Vec)
design_decisions.rationale: ResolutionProblem.objective is already Option<OptimizationObjective> — singular. A structure logically has at most one optimization direction. Multiple minimize/maximize in one structure would be contradictory. If multiple are present, last-wins is simplest. This avoids unnecessary indirection — the compiler output maps directly to what the solver consumes.

design_decisions.decision: Reuse OptimizationObjective from reify-types directly rather than defining a new CompiledObjective wrapper
design_decisions.rationale: OptimizationObjective already wraps CompiledExpr (the compiled form). No additional metadata (like span or id) is needed on the objective at the TopologyTemplate level. A wrapper struct would add indirection with no benefit. The span is preserved in the MemberDecl for diagnostics.

design_decisions.decision: No compile-time type checking on minimize/maximize expressions (no warning for non-numeric objectives)
design_decisions.rationale: Unlike constraints (which must produce Bool and get a warning if not), optimization objectives should accept any numeric expression (Scalar, Real, Int). The solver already interprets values as f64 via as_f64(). Adding type checking can be done later as a diagnostic improvement without changing the compiler structure.

design_decisions.decision: Wiring objectives from TopologyTemplate into ResolutionProblem in the eval layer is out of scope
design_decisions.rationale: The eval layer currently hardcodes 'objective: None'. Threading objectives through requires changes to the eval graph, demand computation, and possibly the Engine API. This is a separate task — this task focuses on getting objectives from source code into the compiled output.

reuse.how: minimize_declaration and maximize_declaration use the identical seq('keyword', field('expr', $._expression)) shape. Copy and replace keyword string.

lower_minimize() and lower_maximize() follow the same pattern: extract expr field via child_by_field_name('expr'), call lower_expr(), construct struct with span and content_hash. Only difference: return MinimizeDecl/MaximizeDecl instead of ConstraintDecl, and no label field.
minimize_declaration and maximize_declaration match arms follow the identical error-check → lower → push-to-members pattern, routing to MemberDecl::Minimize/Maximize.
Minimize/Maximize handling reuses compile_expr() call, but wraps result in OptimizationObjective::{Minimize,Maximize} and assigns to objective instead of pushing to constraints vec.
Already defined with Minimize(CompiledExpr) and Maximize(CompiledExpr) variants. Import and use directly — no new types needed in reify-types.
Add .objective() builder method following the same self-consuming builder pattern used by .param(), .constraint(), .realization(). Default to None.
Parse source string → compile → assert on compiled output structure. Reuse this approach for minimize/maximize compiler tests.
